data.title: Why Bitcoin Is Sliding Toward $90K amid Trump–Greenland Tensions
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is sliding toward $90,000 as investors grow cautious amid rising geopolitical tensions. Comments involving U.S. President Donald Trump and Greenland have weighed on market sentiment, leading some traders to reduce exposure to riskier assets like cryptocurrencies. BTC is down over 1% in today's trading, extending its losses to roughly 5% over the past five days.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What's Driving the Plunge?

Over the weekend, Trump warned he would impose a 10% tariff on imports from Denmark, Norway, Sweden, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Finland, and the UK starting February 1, with the rate rising to 25% on June 1 unless an agreement is reached on his proposal to bring Greenland under U.S. control.

Trump said on Monday that he plans to discuss Greenland during this week's World Economic Forum in Davos. While he did not name who he would meet, leaders from several European countries are expected to attend.

This led to negative sentiment around cryptocurrencies. Bitcoin dropped sharply, sliding from nearly $96,000 to just over $90,000 in the last few days. The second-largest cryptocurrency, Ethereum (ETH-USD), is also down nearly 6% over the past five days. Meanwhile, gold hit a fresh all-time high as investors rushed toward safe havens.

Bitcoin Volatile as Bank of Japan Rate Decision Looms

Investors also became nervous and sold Bitcoin ahead of the Bank of Japan's upcoming interest rate decision.

The central bank signaled it could raise interest rates if the economy and inflation stay strong, which pushed Japanese bond yields sharply higher. Rising bond yields make safer investments more attractive, causing investors to move money away from risky assets like Bitcoin.

Year-to-date, BTC is up by 5.54%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: 'Sales and Earnings to Shrink in 2026' TD Cowen Says of Novo Nordisk Stock (NVO)
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Michael Nedelcovych lowered his model estimates for Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk (NVO), citing fierce competition in the incretin market, particularly from Eli Lilly (LLY). He predicts sales and earnings could shrink starting in 2026, with NVO stock's valuation at risk due to the anticipated U.S. patent expiration of semaglutide in 2032.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Semaglutide is Novo's blockbuster type 2 diabetes and anti-obesity drug. It acts like the natural GLP-1 (glucagon-like peptide-1) hormone to control blood sugar and aids in weight loss by curbing hunger and boosting fullness. It is marketed as Ozempic, Rybelsus, and Wegovy.

On January 16, NVO stock rallied more than 9% after early data showed strong first-week U.S. demand for Wegovy. Additionally, Britain's Medicines and Healthcare Products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) approved a higher weekly dose of the Wegovy injection, up to 7.2 milligrams from 2.4 milligrams.

Analyst's View on NVO Stock

Nedelcovych recently cut his price target from $105 to $70 (Street-high target), implying 12.3% upside potential from current levels. Despite the lowered estimates, he maintained a Buy rating, highlighting five key catalysts that could influence 2026 performance, though he sees a narrow path ahead.

  • Wegovy Pill launch
  • REDEFINE 4 readout – a Phase III trial pitting CagriSema against Lilly's Zepbound,
  • CagriSema's first Phase III diabetes data
  • Ozempic trends
  • Ziltivekimab's pivotal studies

Why Novo Faces a Narrower Path Forward

Nedelcovych warns that Novo Nordisk's dominance in diabetes and obesity markets is fading amid intensifying competition. He believes that after years of blockbuster success with Ozempic and Wegovy (both powered by semaglutide), 2026 could mark a pivotal downturn.

In his updated model, Nedelcovych projected 2026 sales at DKK 290 billion, showing a stark 5% drop from his prior estimate of DKK 334 billion and below consensus of DKK 296 billion. Moreover, EPS is projected to fall by 14% to DKK 19.30, significantly lower compared to his previous DKK 25.25 estimate and consensus of DKK 21.26.

Furthermore, his analysis shows that 2026 could mark the start of pressure on Novo's P/E (price-to-earnings) multiple. This is due to investors' anticipated expiry of semaglutide patent in the U.S. While Novo Nordisk's pipeline offers hope, Nedelcovych warns that weak commercial execution in this make-or-break year raises the stakes.

Nedelcovych stated that the incoming CEO has no radical changes, only sharper execution of proven strategies. He also acknowledged that 2026 might be premature to judge this approach, and the above-listed key catalysts would reveal if Novo can rebuild the lost momentum.

Is Novo Nordisk a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Analysts remain divided on Novo Nordisk's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, NVO stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on four Buys, three Holds, and one Sell rating. The average Novo Nordisk price target of $54.67 implies 12.2% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, NVO shares have lost 20.8%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Baidu's (BIDU) Ernie AI Assistant Tops 200M Users as China's AI Race Heats Up
data.description:
Baidu's (BIDU) push into consumer-facing artificial intelligence (AI) is gaining traction. The company's AI assistant, powered by its Ernie large language model, has now surpassed 200 million monthly active users, according to the Wall Street Journal. The milestone reflects how quickly China's tech giants are racing to embed AI into everyday digital life.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ernie Assistant Gains Traction Through Deep Integrations

Ernie Assistant is a key part of Baidu's AI strategy. It is built directly into the company's main Baidu search app and available on desktop, giving it a huge user base from the start.

Its rapid growth comes from being built into many of China's most popular apps and services, including platforms like JD.com (JD), Meituan (MPNGF), and Trip.com (TCOM). Ernie Assistant is trained to book flights and hotels, order food delivery, provide health and legal information, and generate videos, images, and summaries.

Users can also choose which AI model they want to run, Baidu's own Ernie model or alternatives such as DeepSeek. The assistant also links Baidu Maps and Baidu Health, expanding its reach into navigation, wellness, and daily utility tasks.

China's AI Race Intensifies

Baidu may have been one of the first major Chinese tech companies to launch a ChatGPT‑style chatbot in 2023, but competition has grown much tougher since then.

Alibaba (BABA) is quickly growing its Qwen chatbot, which hit 100 million monthly users just two months after launch. It is now being built into Alibaba's huge consumer ecosystem, letting it handle tasks for users across shopping, payments, and cloud services.

Also, Tencent (TCEHY) and other companies are pouring money into building core AI models and new AI applications.

Further, newer models like DeepSeek and ByteDance's Doubao have become strong rivals, challenging Baidu's early lead in China's generative AI market.

Is BIDU a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BIDU stock based on 10 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months. Further, the average Baidu price target of $163.14 per share implies 9.1% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Intel (INTC) Q4 Earnings on the Deck: Options Traders Brace for an 8.82% Swing
data.description:
Chip giant Intel Corporation (INTC) is set to report its fourth-quarter 2025 results after the market closes on Thursday, January 22, and the options market is expecting a volatile reaction. Based on options pricing, traders are expecting an 8.82% move in either direction in INTC stock in reaction to Q4 2025 results. This implied move is much higher than the stock's average post-earnings move (in absolute terms) of 4.61% over the past four quarters.

data.title: Netflix (NFLX) Is About to Report Q4 Earnings. Here Is What to Expect
data.description:
Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) is scheduled to announce its fourth-quarter results after the market closes on January 20. Heading into Q4 earnings, there have been concerns about the company's takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), with the potential deal facing competing bids from Paramount Skydance (PSKY). Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Netflix to report earnings per share (EPS) of $0.55 for Q4 2025, reflecting a 28% year-over-year growth. Revenue is expected to increase by 17% to $11.97 billion.

data.title: M&A News: Beazley Stock Rallies on Improved Takeover Offer by Zurich
data.description:
Shares of U.K. cyber insurer Beazley (GB:BEZ) rallied more than 40% after Switzerland's Zurich Insurance (CH:ZURN) made an improved bid of £7.67 billion ($10.3 billion) to acquire the company. Zurich said that it has now made an offer of 1,280 pence per share in cash to acquire Beazley, reflecting a 56% premium to BEZ's closing stock price on January 16, 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here Is Why Zurich Is Keen to Acquire Beazley

Following its previous offers, on January 4, 2026, Zurich submitted a proposal to Beazley's board to acquire the company at 1,230 pence per share in cash. However, on January 16, Beazley's board turned down the offer, saying it significantly undervalued the company.

Zurich believes that its proposal provides Beazley shareholders "immediate and certain cash value for their investment" at a level that surpasses what the latter could achieve through the execution of its strategy, as set out at the Capital Markets Day on November 25, 2025. The company also believes that its offer fully reflects Beazley's fundamental value.

Notably, Zurich, a dominant player in commercial lines insurance, believes that the proposed acquisition would create a global leader in specialty insurance. It believes that a potential deal would combine two highly complementary businesses and establish a leading global specialty platform, leveraging Beazley's Lloyd's of London presence. 

Is Beazley Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Beazley scores a Strong Buy consensus rating based on nine unanimous Buys. The average BEZ stock price target of 1,017.22 pence per share indicates 13% downside risk.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Drop as Trade Tensions Hit Markets After Holiday
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down ahead of Tuesday's session after investors reacted to President Donald Trump's comments on Greenland and new tariff threats. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 1.21%, 0.94%, and 1.05%, respectively, at 12:09 a.m. EDT on January 20.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a Truth Social post on Saturday, Trump said the U.S. would impose an additional 10% tariff on eight countries, including the United Kingdom, starting on February 1. Afterward, the rate will rise to 25% on June 1. The tariffs will remain in place until the U.S. secures a deal to acquire Greenland.

Monday night marked the first full chance for U.S. investors to react to the trade news, as markets were closed earlier in the day for the Martin Luther King Jr. holiday.

Last week, the S&P 500 fell 0.4%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average lost 0.3%. The Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.7% for the week. This came despite a brief lift in chip stocks tied to artificial intelligence after Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) posted strong fourth-quarter results on Thursday.

Earnings will also be in focus this week, with several companies reporting results, including Netflix (NFLX), 3M (MMM), United Airlines (UAL), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Charles Schwab (SCHW), and Intel (INTC).

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Micron Stock (MU) Hits a New 52-Week High – Will the Rally Continue?
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) stock continues its impressive run, hitting a new 52-week high of $365.81 on January 16, 2026, amid aggressive expansion and booming artificial intelligence (AI) demand. This rally builds on a 231% gain over the past year, raising questions about its continuity in a competitive semiconductor landscape.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AI tailwinds and manufacturing scale have positioned Micron strongly, but risks from potential deal delays, geopolitical tensions in Taiwan, intense rivalry from Samsung (SSNLF) and SK Hynix, and broader economic slowdowns could temper hyperscaler spending.

Key Catalysts Behind Micron's Recent Rally

  • The company broke ground on a massive $100 billion semiconductor manufacturing plant in upstate New York. Micron executives describe this Onondaga County facility near Syracuse as the largest semiconductor plant in the US. It is slated to be fully operational by 2030 and create 50,000 jobs in New York state.
  • Director Liu Teyin M purchased 23,200 common shares on January 13-14, as seen in a Form 4 filing. This move signals high insider confidence in the company's future potential.
  • Micron signed a letter of intent (LOI) to buy a fabrication plant from Taiwan's Powerchip Semiconductor Manufacturing ‌Corp. for $1.8 billion, expected to close in the second half of 2026. Micron will gradually boost DRAM (Dynamic Random Access Memory) production at the site, with major wafer output starting late 2027. Its location near Micron's current Taiwan facilities improves long-term efficiency. Micron and Powerchip will team up on chip assembly post-production, while Micron supports Powerchip's older DRAM lines during the switch.

The massive AI-driven demand from data centers and hyperscalers has sparked memory chip shortages and driven up prices. Micron plans to grow its production capacity via the Taiwan fab purchase and its upcoming New York plant.

Micron Stock Movement

Over the past year, MU shares have soared more than 231%, with a sharp rally toward the end of 2025. This momentum comes from strong demand for Micron's high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and DRAM chips, fueled by AI applications. Moreover, consistent quarterly earnings beats, improving profitability, and rising global memory prices have further boosted the stock.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Tightens Its Grip on South Korea's EV Market with Aggressive Pricing
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is strengthening its position in South Korea, one of Asia's most competitive electric vehicle (EV) markets, by slashing prices and triggering a full-scale price war. Recent price cuts on the Model 3 and Model Y have made them more affordable, helping TSLA pull ahead of major rivals in both sales and market share.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

With this move, Korea might be entering a "China-style EV price war," where Tesla keeps cutting prices to protect its market share, pushing rivals to follow suit and pressuring industry profits.

Price Cuts Make Tesla's Vehicles Affordable

Tesla sharply cut the price of the Model 3, lowering the Standard RWD version to 41.99 million won (about $28,500) from 59.9 million won. With subsidies, the final price drops into the low‑30‑million‑won range.

The company also reduced prices on the Model 3 Performance and rolled out discounts on the Model Y, bringing both models below the 40 million won threshold after incentives.

These cuts are significant as most EVs in Korea, especially models from Hyundai (HYMLF), Kia (KIMTF), BMW (BAMXF), and Mercedes (MBGAF), usually cost between 40 million and 50 million won. Thus, Tesla's aggressive pricing suddenly makes its cars some of the most cost-competitive options in the country.

Tesla Sees Strong Sales Surge in 2025

According to market tracker CarIsYou, Tesla's sales in Korea more than doubled last year. The company sold 59,949 units, reflecting a 101.5% increase from the previous year. The Model Y alone sold 50,405 units, becoming the top-selling imported vehicle in the country.

Several factors are driving Tesla's momentum, such as:

  • Affordability: After subsidies, Tesla's pricing now rivals or beats local EVs.
  • Brand appeal: Younger buyers in their 30s and 40s prefer Tesla for its tech-forward image and Autopilot features.
  • Feature advantage: Even with some feature trade-offs in the lower-priced Model 3, Tesla still offers strong value relative to rivals.
  • Market timing: Overall, EV demand has softened in Korea, making price a decisive factor.

Is Tesla a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the last three months. At $397.47, the average Tesla price target implies a 9.15% downside risk. The stock has gained 33.19% over the past six months.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: 'Temporary' Iron Ore Discounts Won't Hit BHP's Pricing Power, Says RBC
data.description:
BHP Group Limited (BHP) (AU:BHP) reported record iron ore production in the first six months of the current fiscal year, while noting it has accepted lower prices during negotiations with China. Nonetheless, RBC five-star analyst Kaan Peker views the discounts as "optical, temporary, and economically bound," noting that they do not signal any deterioration in the mining giant's underlying pricing power. Following the announcement, BHP shares in Australia fell by almost 2% today.
